posts.text: “Beirut cannot remain a city governed by ambiguity,” states Fouad Makhzoumi. In a guest essay the politician writes that “the Lebanese state faces a clear choice: assert its authority in the capital, or continue to cede it to forces beyond its control” https://www.economist.com/by-invitation/2026/03/22/a-plan-to-reclaim-beirut?taid=69c16ce37f3b6800019dfcd8&utm_campaign=trueanthem&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter
posts.author: TheEconomist

posts.text:
BREAKING: President Trump says his envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner have spoken with a senior Iranian official, with potential "deal" terms emerging, per Axios.

President Trump on potential deal terms:

1. Iran committed not to pursue nuclear weapons or to enrich uranium, and to hand over its existing stockpiles

2. Iran agreed to be "low key on the missiles"

3. Iran also agreed to reopen the strait of Hormuz

4. "If they carry through with that it will end the conflict. They want to make a deal. We want to make a deal too. If this happens it is a great start for Iran to build itself back," Trump said

The US notified Israel on Monday about the peace talks with Iran.

We await comment from Iran.
posts.author: KobeissiLetter

posts.text:
I’ve seen posts lately about the importance of critical minerals in Tomahawk missiles.

 Here is the simplified breakdown of the estimated reduction or replacement potential for each critical mineral in a tomahawk missle using Turbostratic Fractal Graphene 
 * Copper (Cu) & Silver (Ag): 95% – 100%
 * Tantalum (Ta): 95% – 100%
 * Graphite (C): 100%
 * Gallium (Ga): 80% – 100%
 * Aluminum (Al): 80% – 95%
 * Titanium (Ti): 70% – 90%
 * Vanadium (V): 70% – 90%
 * Cobalt (Co) & Molybdenum (Mo): 10% – 20%

Conclusion: $hg $hgraf is going to dramatically reduce USA reliance on critical minerals. 

@DeptofWar @ENERGY @CMCoe @RealDougCasey 

With the Middle East conflict in the news and fears about China’s domination of critical minerals supply and Iran’s success with drones.  It would seem imperative that the USA government fast track the development of a USA GEIC in Texas and partner with $hg $hgraf to innovate with Turbostratic Fractal Graphene.  It’s a matter of national security.  Further, it’s imperative for all NATO countries to upgrade military hardware with this new graphene material.
posts.author: BambroughKevin
